title: Supplementary Modules to the Core Cultural Formulation Interview (CFI)
description: Guidelines for Implementing the CFI Supplementary Modules

fields.label: *Can you tell me more about how you understand your [PROBLEM]?
What did you know about your [PROBLEM] before it affected you?
Had you ever had anything like your [PROBLEM] before?
Do you know anyone else, or heard of anyone else, with this [PROBLEM]?
*Can you tell me what you think caused your [PROBLEM]?
Have your ideas about the cause of the [PROBLEM] changed?
*What do people in your family, friends, or others in your community think caused the [PROBLEM]?
How do you think your [PROBLEM] affects your body?
What usually happens to people who have this [PROBLEM]?
Do you consider your [PROBLEM] to be serious?
How concerned are other people in your family, friends or community about your having this [PROBLEM]?
What do you think is the best way to deal with this kind of problem?
How has your [PROBLEM] affected your ability to interact with your family and other people in your life?
How has your [PROBLEM] affected your ability to work?
How has your [PROBLEM] affected your financial situation?
How has your [PROBLEM] affected your ability to take part in community and social activities?
How has your [PROBLEM] affected your ability to enjoy everyday life?
Which of these concerns are most troubling to you?
Which of these concerns are most troubling to your family and to other people in your life?
Who are the most important people in your life at present?
Is there anyone in particular whom you trust and can talk with about your [PROBLEM]?
Which of your family members, friends, or other important people in your life know about your [PROBLEM]?
What ideas do your family and friends have about the nature of your [PROBLEM]?
Are there people who do not know about your [PROBLEM]?
What advice have family members and friends given you about your [PROBLEM]?
Do your family, friends, and other people in your life treat you differently because of your [PROBLEM]?
What kinds of help or support were you expecting from family or friends?
Have any family members or friends helped you get treatment for your [PROBLEM]?
What would your family and friends think about your coming here to receive treatment?
Would you like your family, friends, or others to be part of your treatment?
How would involving family or friends make a difference in your treatment?
How are the people around you affected by these [STRESSORS]?
How do you cope with these [STRESSORS]?
What have other people suggested about coping with these [STRESSORS]?
What else could be done about these [STRESSORS]?
Have you experienced discrimination or been treated badly as a result of your background or identity?
Do you identify with any particular spiritual, religious or moral tradition?
Do you belong to a congregation or community associated with that tradition?
What are the spiritual, religious or moral tradition backgrounds of your family members?
Have you been in conflict with others over spiritual, religious or moral issues?
Where were you born?
Where were your parents and grandparents born?
How would you describe your family’s national, ethnic, and/or racial background?
In terms of your background, how do you usually describe yourself to people outside your community?
Which part of your background do you feel closest to?
What languages do you speak fluently?
What languages did you speak growing up?
What languages are spoken at home?
What languages do you use at work or school?
What language would you prefer to use in getting health care?
What languages do you read?
When did you come to this country?
What made you decide to leave your country of origin?
How has your life changed since coming here?
What do you miss about the place or community you came from?
How has migration influenced your health or that of your family?
Do you identify with any particular religious, moral or spiritual tradition?
Do your family members share your spiritual, religious or moral traditions?
Do you feel that your [GENDER] has influenced your [PROBLEM] or your health more generally?
Do you feel that your [GENDER] has influenced your ability to get the kind of health care you need?
Do you feel that your sexual orientation has influenced your [PROBLEM] or your health more generally?
What are the most important aspects of your background or identity in relation to [PROBLEM]?
*Can you tell me more about how you are trying to cope with [PROBLEM] at this time?
*Can you tell me more about how you tried to cope with the [PROBLEM] or with similar problems in the past?
*Have you told a family member about your [PROBLEM]?
*Have you told a friend or co-worker about your [PROBLEM]?
Are you involved in activities that involve other people related to a spiritual, religious or moral tradition?
Have you ever tried to get help for your [PROBLEM] from your general doctor?
What were the circumstances that led to your coming here for treatment for your [PROBLEM]?
What help are you hoping to get here [at this clinic] for your [PROBLEM]?
What kind of experiences have you had with clinicians in the past?
Have you had difficulties with clinicians in the past?
How did you feel about your relationship with the patient?
What was the quality of communication with the patient?
Are there aspects of your own identity that may influence your attitudes toward this patient?
Do you feel you are like other children/youth your age?
Do you sometimes feel different from other children/youth your age?
Do you feel your family is different from other families?
Do you use different languages?
Does your name have any special meaning for you?
Is there something special about you that you like or that you are proud of?
What do you like about being a child/youth at home?
What don’t you like about being a child/youth at home?
Who is there to support you when you feel you need it?
What do your parents or grandparents expect from a child/youth your age?
What do your school teachers expect from a child/youth your age?
*IF INDIVIDUAL HAS SIBLINGS* : What do your siblings expect from a child/youth your age?
What do other children/youth your age expect from a child/youth your age?
When is a youth considered ready to become an adult in your family or community?
When is a youth considered ready to become an adult according to your school teachers?
What is good or difficult about becoming a young woman or a young man in your family?
How do you feel about “growing up” or becoming an adult?
In what ways are your life and responsibilities different from the life and responsibilities of your parents?
How would you describe a person of your age?
How does your experience of aging compare to that of your friends and relatives who are of a similar age?
Is there anything about being your age that helps you cope with your current life situation?
How does being older influence your [PROBLEM]?
Are there ways that being older influences how you deal with your [PROBLEM]?
Have you had health problems due to your age?
How have your health conditions or the treatments for your health conditions affected your [PROBLEM]?
Are there any ways that your health conditions or treatments influence how you deal with your [PROBLEM]?
Are there things that are important to you that you are unable to do because of your health or age?
Who do you rely on for help or support in your daily life in general?
How has [PROBLEM] affected your relationships with family and friends?
Are you receiving the amount and kind of support you expected?
Do the people you rely on share your view of your [PROBLEM]?
How has your age affected how health providers treat you?
What is your country of origin?
How long have you been living here in __________ (HOST COUNTRY)?
When and with whom did you leave _____________ (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN)?
Why did you leave ___________ (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN)?
Of the persons important/close to you, who stayed behind?
Do you or your family miss anything about your way of life in (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN)?
Do you have concerns about relatives that remain in (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN)?
Do relatives in (COUNTRY OF ORIGIN) have any expectations of you?
Has coming to [HOST COUNTRY] resulted in something positive for you or your family?
What hopes and plans do you have for you and your family in the coming years?
How long have you been taking care of [INDIVIDUAL RECEIVING CARE]?
How are you connected to [INDIVIDUAL RECEIVING CARE]?
How do you help him/her with the [PROBLEM] or with day-to-day activities?
What is most rewarding about helping him/her?
What is most challenging about helping him/her?
How, if at all, has his/her [PROBLEM] changed your relationship?
Are there any cultural traditions that influence how you approach helping [INDIVIDUAL RECEIVING CARE]?
[ *IF CAREGIVER IS A FAMILY MEMBER:* ] How do you, as a family, cope with this [PROBLEM]?
How do you feel about how much or how little others are helping with his/her [PROBLEM]?
How do you see yourself helping to provide care to [INDIVIDUAL RECEIVING CARE] now and in the future?
[ *IF UNCLEAR* :] How do you see yourself helping with the care that he/she receives in this clinic?
